agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"POST /validate","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"No circuit breaker protecting the service from SmartyStreets outages. A sustained SmartyStreets degradation will cause thread/connection pool exhaustion as requests pile up waiting on the 3s timeout."},{"from":"/health","to":"SmartyStreets API","status":"risk","issue":"Health endpoint couples liveness to an external dependency. If SmartyStreets has a transient blip, orchestrators (K8s) may restart healthy pods, causing cascading failures. Liveness and readiness probes should be separated."}],"contract_compliance":"No validation of SmartyStreets response schema. If they change their API contract, the service will fail in unpredictable ways with no clear error signal.","data_flow_integrity":"Input address is not logged and raw API response is not logged, making it impossible to reproduce or audit validation decisions after the fact."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["No request ID / correlation ID in any log line — cannot trace a single request across logs","No duration logged for the SmartyStreets call — cannot identify latency degradation from logs alone","Input address not logged — cannot reproduce a failed validation","Raw SmartyStreets response not logged (even at DEBUG level) — cannot determine if a bad result was a service bug or upstream data issue","No structured logging format mentioned — grep-based debugging will be brittle"]},"metrics":{"coverage":"minimal — single counter with no dimensional breakdown","gaps":["validation_requests_total lacks a result label (valid/invalid/error) — cannot alert on spike in invalid or error rates","No latency histogram (e.g. validation_duration_seconds) — cannot set SLO on p50/p95/p99 or detect SmartyStreets degradation","No in-flight request gauge — cannot detect connection pool saturation","No error counter broken down by error type (timeout, 4xx, 5xx, connection refused) — cannot distinguish failure modes in dashboards"]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed trace context propagation — if this service is called by other services, there is no way to correlate upstream and downstream spans","No span emitted for the SmartyStreets outbound call"]},"debugging_capability":"Poor. An on-call engineer at 3am seeing elevated error rates has almost nothing to work with: no request IDs to grep, no input data to reproduce, no latency data to confirm whether SmartyStreets is slow, and no error-type breakdown to narrow the failure mode."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"SmartyStreets returns 5xx or is completely down","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Implement a circuit breaker (e.g. 50% error rate over 30s window → open). When open, return a clear 503 with a machine-readable error code so callers can degrade gracefully instead of waiting 3s per request."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets latency degrades (responds in 2.5s instead of normal 200ms)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Without a latency histogram there is no alert. Add validation_duration_seconds histogram and alert when p95 exceeds 1s. Consider an adaptive timeout or deadline propagation from the caller."},{"scenario":"SmartyStreets rate-limits the service (429)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff ONLY for 429/503. Do NOT retry 4xx client errors. Log the rate-limit headers. Surface a specific metric label for rate-limited requests."},{"scenario":"Malformed or unexpected response body from SmartyStreets","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add defensive deserialization with explicit error handling and logging of the raw body (truncated) when parsing fails."},{"scenario":"Pod receives traffic before SmartyStreets connectivity is confirmed","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Separate Kubernetes liveness probe (always 200 if process is up) from readiness probe (200 only when SmartyStreets is reachable). Current /health conflates both."}],"retry_config":"Not mentioned. No retry policy exists for transient SmartyStreets failures. Must add with exponential backoff, jitter, and a cap of 2 retries max to stay within caller timeout budgets.","timeout_config":"3s timeout exists — reasonable as a default, but there is no deadline propagation from the caller. If the caller has a 2s budget, this service will still wait 3s. Consider accepting an optional deadline header.","circuit_breakers":"None. This is a must-have before production. Without it, SmartyStreets outages will propagate upstream.","graceful_degradation":"None defined. The service should have a degradation strategy: when SmartyStreets is unavailable, should it reject with 503, return a 'validation unavailable' soft result, or fall back to regex-based format checking? This is a product decision that must be made before launch."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Redeploy previous container image. Acceptable for a stateless service with no database migrations. Verify that any callers using feature flags or routing rules are also reverted.","monitoring_coverage":"Insufficient. A single undifferentiated counter provides no actionable signal. Minimum viable monitoring requires: (1) request rate by result, (2) latency histogram, (3) error rate by type, (4) circuit breaker state metric, (5) SmartyStreets availability from the service's perspective.","on_call_documentation":"Not mentioned. Requires a runbook covering: how to verify SmartyStreets status, how to toggle the circuit breaker manually, how to read the logs, escalation contacts at SmartyStreets, and expected traffic patterns.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":[],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Add Prometheus histogram metric validation_duration_seconds","Add result label to validation_requests_total","Configure circuit breaker thresholds (error rate %, window, cooldown)","Separate liveness and readiness probe endpoints"],"infra_provisioning":["Grafana dashboard for the new metrics","Alert rules for error rate, latency p95, and circuit breaker open state"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":false,"data_preserving":false,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"Stateless service, no database."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Metrics and logging are insufficient to operate this service","reason":"A single counter with no labels, no latency histogram, no request IDs, no error-type breakdown, and no tracing means the on-call engineer cannot diagnose any production incident. You cannot set an SLO, cannot alert on degradation, and cannot trace a request. This is below the minimum bar for production observability.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"No circuit breaker and no degradation strategy means SmartyStreets failures propagate unchecked","reason":"Without a circuit breaker, a SmartyStreets outage causes every inbound request to block for 3s and fail. At any meaningful traffic volume this exhausts connection pools and cascades upstream. Combined with no metrics to detect it, this is an operational blind spot. Shaq should add a circuit breaker and the team should decide the degradation policy before shipping.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":55,"high_confidence_areas":["Observability gaps are clear and objectively verifiable","Resilience gaps (no circuit breaker, no retry) are definitive","Rollback story is adequate for a stateless service"],"low_confidence_areas":["Unknown traffic volume — severity of connection pool exhaustion depends on QPS","Unknown caller timeout budgets — 3s may or may not be appropriate","Unknown whether SmartyStreets SLA/uptime history justifies circuit breaker urgency","Unknown deployment platform details (K8s assumed from health check pattern)"],"assumptions":["Service is deployed on Kubernetes or similar orchestrator with probe-based health checking","SmartyStreets is the only external dependency","Service is stateless with no database","No existing Grafana dashboards or alert rules cover this service yet"]}}
